title: Choose HNSW or IVFFlat by workload shape
description: Pick HNSW for high recall at query time and IVFFlat for fast builds and lower memory, matching the workload.
body: Use when selecting an ANN index type for a vector column. Do not default to one index for every workload; HNSW gives high recall and low query latency but builds slowly and uses more memory, while IVFFlat builds fast and is memory-light but needs enough training data and a tuned probe count to reach the same recall. Actions: pick HNSW for read-heavy, recall-critical search; pick IVFFlat for large, frequently-rebuilt datasets where build cost dominates; benchmark recall@k for both on a sample. Validation signals include a recall@k curve meeting target and build time fitting the maintenance window. Risk boundary: HNSW build memory grows with graph degree, so confirm the maintenance_work_mem budget before building on a large table or escalate to a phased build.
